title: Pleopeltis alborufula
intro: Pleopeltis alborufula, also known as the Red Stemmed Polypody, is a species of fern belonging to the family Polypodiaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pleopeltis alborufula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pleopeltis alborufula include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions and to promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Pleopeltis alborufula belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody ferns. This plant is a type of epiphytic fern, which means it grows on other plants or objects for support. It is classified under the order Polypodiales, which is a large and diverse group of ferns. The species Pleopeltis alborufula is a member of the genus Pleopeltis, which is found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. Further classification of Pleopeltis alborufula may involve specific infraspecific ranks such as subspecies, variety or form.
